pi_items.metadata_value: 555 Timer Astable Multivibrator circuit for blinking LED
pi_items.verified_value: 555 Timer Astable Multivibrator is a real, well-documented circuit used for LED blinking applications
pi_items.source: Verified via web search on multiple electronics reference sites including electronics-tutorials.ws, circuitbasics.com, allaboutcircuits.com

pi_items.metadata_value: U1: 555 Timer IC (8 pins) with Pin 1 (GND), Pin 4 (RESET), Pin 7 (DISCHARGE), Pin 8 (VCC), Pin 2 (TRIGGER), Pin 6 (THRESHOLD), Pin 3 (OUTPUT)
pi_items.verified_value: 555 Timer IC has 8 pins with these exact functions and names per standard documentation
pi_items.source: Verified via Wikipedia, jameco.com, circuitbread.com, electronicsforu.com

pi_items.metadata_value: Pin 2 (TRIGGER) and Pin 6 (THRESHOLD) connected together for astable operation
pi_items.verified_value: This is the correct configuration for astable multivibrator operation in 555 timer circuits
pi_items.source: Verified via electronics-tutorials.ws and multiple astable circuit references

pi_items.verified_value: Netlist in setup_task.sh creates: U1 (1 IC), R1/R2/R3 (3 resistors), C1 (1 capacitor), D1 (1 LED) - matches claim
These values are reasonable for a 555 astable LED blinking circuit; 1k and 470k control oscillation frequency, 1uF capacitor is typical, 220 ohm is standard LED current-limiting resistor
pi_items.source: Verified via eleccircuit.com, elonics.org, homemade-circuits.com; 220 ohm LED limiting resistor confirmed as standard by gsnetwork.com

pi_items.verified_value: 220 ohm resistor is a standard value for LED current limiting in 555 timer circuits, limiting current to safe levels below 20mA
pi_items.source: Verified via gsnetwork.com and multiple LED limiting resistor references

pi_items.metadata_value: draw.io has an Electrical shape library with resistor, capacitor, IC, LED, and connector symbols
pi_items.verified_value: draw.io has built-in electrical shape library for circuit diagrams; custom libraries also available for enhanced symbols
pi_items.source: Verified via drawio-app.com and multiple GitHub repositories (bzarek/draw.io-electrical, NicklasVraa/Draw-io-ECE)

pi_items.metadata_value: draw.io can export diagrams as PNG images
pi_items.verified_value: draw.io supports PNG export via File > Export as > PNG with various quality options
pi_items.source: Verified via draw.io official documentation at drawio.com/doc/faq/export-to-png

pi_items.verified_value: Expected labels should be ["R1", "R2", "R3", "C1", "D1", "555", "1k", "470k", "1uF", "220"]; task.json is MISSING "R3" and "220"
pi_items.source: Contradiction found: (1) setup_task.sh creates R3 and sets its value to 220 Ohm (lines 33-34); (2) export_result.sh expects ["r1", "r2", "r3", "c1", "d1", "555", "1k", "470k", "1uf", "220"] (line 116); (3) verifier.py checks for key_labels in ["555", "1k", "1uf", "470k", "220"] (line 91)

pi_items.verified_value: Path matches the location where setup_task.sh creates the netlist (line 26)
Path is consistent with export_result.sh (line 8) and task setup expectations
Path is consistent with export_result.sh (line 9) and task setup expectations

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a 555 Timer Astable Multivibrator circuit schematic for a blinking LED, which is a real, well-documented circuit type. The 555 timer IC has 8 pins with verified functions. The circuit uses 3 resistors (1k, 470k, 220 ohm), 1 capacitor (1uF), and 1 LED, which are verified component values appropriate for this application. All component values and circuit topology match standard 555 timer astable configurations found in authoritative electronics references. Draw.io supports electrical symbols and PNG export as required. CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: The task.json metadata field 'required_labels' omits "R3" and "220", but these components are explicitly created in the setup script and expected by both the export analysis script and the verifier.py evaluation script, creating an inconsistency in task specification.
